MINI MINUTES: Appearances: B. Douglas Robbins, Esq. for Plaintiff ENA North Beach Inc.; Ann Draper and Thomas LaLanne, Esq. for Defendants 524 Union Street and Beverly Smucha.

The Court distributes its written tentative ruling reiterating its position that pursuant to CCP Sec. 916(a) it lacks jurisdiction to proceed in light of an appeal having been filed. After discussion and further consideration, the Court adopts it tentative ruling finding a want of jurisdiction. The motions are off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: April Heveroh, April Heveroh & Assoc., CSR #8759, 510-406-6651. (D306)

Ex Parte Hearing On Motion To Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Levy
Off Calendar Sep-20-2017 Continued To Sep-21-17 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: B. Douglas Robbins, Esq. for Plaintiff ENA North Beach Inc.; Thomas LaLanne, Esq. for Defendants 524 Union Street and Beverly Smucha.

The Court immediately addresses the issue as to whether the Court has jurisdiction to hear these matters in light of the appeal that has been filed, citing Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 916. In light of the provisions of Sec. 916, the Court is of the opinion that the Motion to Quash Writ and Vacate Levy must be heard by the Court of Appeal. There being no objection, the Court so holds.

POST SCRIPT: Subsequent to hearing, Ms. Ann Draper e-mails the Court to request reconsideration, citing C.C.P. Sec. 917.1. Upon consideration, the Court determines to commence further hearing on Sept. 21, 2017 at 2:30 p.m.

Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Barak Smucha (917-287-6029) Defendant/Moving party 524 UNION STREET, not counsel.
No other appearances.

Defendant informs the Court he believes attorney, James Knopf, has abandoned the case. The court informs the party of the tentative ruling rule and announces the tentative ruling and instructs the party to resolve the issue regarding counsel. No further argument. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 524 UNION STREET and BEVERLY SMUCHA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff's objection regarding insufficient notice is denied. Plaintiff opposed the motion on the merits in depth, and the Court finds waiver per Carlton v. Quint 77 Cal.App.4th 690. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Multiple issues of triable fact, including the duration of due diligence period, the scope of counsel advice received by Plaintiff, the existence of a valid conditional use permit, the representations about Le Bordeaux ABC license and sale of alcohol, etc. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
